title: Hello world — starting the lab notebook
description: A first post: why this site exists, what I'm going to write about here, and what the format is going to look like.
bodyMd:
## Why this exists

I've spent the last six years writing code for other people — first at Lipsey's, then at Tri-Core — while shipping side projects in the cracks. *CookJunkie*, *TutorTab*, *Vorbiz*, and *Weekend Plant* all exist because I wanted to see what "solo, end-to-end, no training wheels" actually looked like in production.

This blog is the lab notebook.

## What I'll write about

- **Legacy modernization.** Not the slide-deck version — the "VB-to-.NET-Core without taking down the app" version.
- **AI as a teammate, not a demo.** How I wrap Claude and friends in real ops tooling that audits recipes, generates content, and does the grunt work I used to do by hand.
- **Solo SaaS engineering.** Stripe Connect edge cases, Google Calendar webhook bugs, offline-first sync — the stuff that only hurts once you hit production.
- **Short write-ups of things I learn.** No filler.

## Format

Posts live as JSON files in `blog/` in this repo. Git is the publication history. If I'm working on something and haven't committed it, it doesn't exist yet.

Some posts will be written. Some will be videos. Some will be both — embed at the top, writeup below, pick your medium.

## What I'm working on now

The portfolio itself. Meta, I know. The palette is anchored on `#55D6D0` (bright cyan), with amber as the warm counter-tension. Type is Fraunces paired with Inter. Every page is statically rendered — no database, no admin UI, no auth. Just JSON files and `fs.readdir`.

If you made it this far: there's a [contact form](/contact). Tell me what you're working on.
